channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Jets interview former Titans coach Mike Vrabel for their head coaching job
item.description: The New York Jets interviewed former Tennessee Titans coach Mike Vrabel on Friday for their head coaching vacancy. Vrabel is the second known coaching candidate to meet with the Jets, who announced they completed their interview with him.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dolphins' Tagovailoa 'unlikely' to play Sunday against Jets, Huntley in line for 2nd straight start
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Quarterback Tua Tagovailoa is expected to miss his second straight game when the Dolphins travel to face the New York Jets on Sunday in a matchup with playoff implications, coach Mike McDaniel said Friday.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mailbag: CFP revenue breakdown and future format changes, Big Ten 2025 schedules, the Fox and ESPN impact, ASU’s close call and more
item.description: Through three rounds of the playoff, the Big Ten leads all conferences in revenue generated by the distribution model.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Rugby star Ilona Maher eyes Bristol debut as ticket sales break club record
item.description: BRISTOL, England (AP) — Ticket sales have broken a record ahead of U.S. rugby star Ilona Maher making her debut for the Bristol Bears women's team.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rafael Campos the feel-good winner at Kapalua. New PGA Tour system might limit these tales
item.description: KAPALUA, Hawaii (AP) — The cream-colored envelope arrived at the Puerto Rico home of Rafael Campos on Dec. 23, and there was no mistaking who it was from — Augusta National — or what it contained. That's why he waited a few days to open it and find his invitation to the Masters.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 49ers’ Ricky Pearsall ‘finds positives in darkness’ of rookie season
item.description: Wide receiver Ricky Pearsall expressed extreme gratitude to his 49ers teammates for helping him through the most difficult of rookie seasons.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: US routs Switzerland 7-2 in world junior quarterfinals; Czechia eliminates Canada 4-3
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — Boston College teammates James Hagens and Ryan Leonard each scored twice and the defending champion United States routed Switzerland 7-2 on Thursday in the world junior hockey quarterfinals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White leads No. 22 Illinois to 109-77 blowout at No. 9 Oregon
item.description: EUGENE, Ore. (AP) — Tre White had 20 points and 11 rebounds as No. 22 Illinois made a resounding statement on the road with a 109-77 blowout of ninth-ranked Oregon on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MIMS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saquon Barkley appears to take shot at Giants in new ad after rushing for 2,000 yards
item.description: Giants owner John Mara famously said he's have trouble sleeping if Saquon Barkley joined the Eagles – well, that's what happened, and the running back wants to help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tom Hoge leads at Kapalua where good golf exceeds expectations in PGA Tour opener
item.description: KAPALUA, Hawaii (AP) — Tom Hoge grew up in North Dakota and found the ideal vibe for Kapalua on Thursday, keeping expectations low and riding the momentum of good golf on his way to a 9-under 64 to take a one-shot lead at The Sentry in the PGA Tour season opener.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Postponed Sugar Bowl played with enhanced security about 36 hours after terror attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Georgia and Notre Dame fans packed a plaza adjacent to the Superdome and enjoyed music under clear skies — under the watch of snipers on rooftops — before filtering into the stadium for Thursday's College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl. “It was a lot of fun.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Big Will's Week 18 NFL Picks
item.description: In this edition of NFL picks, Big Will focuses on the final AFC wildcard spot as three teams fight for the 7-seed.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: College football fans launch into 'U-S-A!' chant after moment of silence for victims of New Orleans attack
item.description: Thousands of football fans chanted "U-S-A!" after a moment of silence for those killed and injured in the tragic New Orleans terror attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill to keep trans athletes out of women's sports is top priority in new House rules package
item.description: The House rules package for the 119th Congress includes a bill that would bring about Title IX revisions to ban trans athletes from women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big 12 commissioner calls for 'national standards' for calling targeting after controversial play
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Big 12 commissioner Brett Yormark is calling for more consistency in determining what constitutes a targeting penalty after no call was made on Texas safety Michael Taaffe's high hit on Arizona State receiver Melquan Stovall late in the College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Peac
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inactive Georgia player draws penalty for bizarre move in Sugar Bowl vs. Notre Dame
item.description: Georgia Bulldogs cornerback Parker Jones was penalized on a big play in the second quarter against the Notre Dame Fighting Irish in the Sugar Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Notre Dame draws back-to-back running into the kicker penalties to open Sugar Bowl
item.description: The Notre Dame Fighting Irish got off to a rough start in the Sugar Bowl against the Georgia Bulldogs in a CFP quarterfinal game Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bowl action? There was plenty, and the best came far away from the spotlight of the college playoffs
item.description: Somewhere in the fight-filled, money-chasing hustle that the college bowl season has become, a national champion will emerge. Too bad that so far, hardly any of the games on the road to that national championship have been worth a hoot.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Allstate Sugar Bowl postponed
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—The Caesars Superdome was placed under lockdown Wednesday after a pickup truck drove into a New Year’s crowd about a mile away, killing at least 15 people and injuring dozens, city and federal officials said.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Ben Johnson likely to push for a GM besides Ryan Poles, report says
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Information continues to emerge about the Chicago Bears' looming pursuit of a new head coach. The presumptive top candidate — Ben Johnson — is tied to the latest details suggesting he would want a new general manager if he came to Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Eagles coach Nick Sirianni set to rest Saquon Barkley in finale and end chance at NFL rushing record
item.description: Saquon Barkley had a heart-to-heart talk with his dad that was more coated in heartbreak than hope over the Philadelphia Eagles running back's pursuit of the NFL single-season rushing record.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy Garoppolo to make debut for playoff-bound Rams in place of Matthew Stafford against Seahawks
item.description: Jimmy Garoppolo will take his first snaps for the Rams when he starts in place of Matthew Stafford in playoff-bound Los Angeles' regular-season finale against Seattle on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gauff and Fritz put US in United Cup semifinals. Kazakhstan beats Germany to advance
item.description: Coco Gauff and Taylor Fritz powered the U.S., tournament winner two years ago, into the semifinals of the United Cup mixed teams tennis event on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LeBron James' youngest son Bryce commits to Arizona
item.description: TUCSON, Ariz. (AP) — Bryce James, son of NBA star LeBron James, is heading to Arizona. Bryce James announced his decision Wednesday on Instagram and his father had a quick response. “CONGRATULATIONS MAXIMUS!!,” LeBron James posted.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed after deadly truck attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS — The College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl between Georgia and Notre Dame has been postponed after a truck plowed into a New Year’s crowd about a mile away early Wednesday, killing at least 10 people.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Lenny Randle, major leaguer repeatedly in the spotlight for 12 seasons, dies at 75
item.description: MURRIETA, Calif. (AP) — Lenny Randle, a big league player for 12 seasons who spoke five languages, performed stand-up comedy, was dubbed “The Most Interesting Man in Baseball” and was suspended for punching his Texas Rangers manager, has died. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Saunders scores 30 as BYU wins Big 12 opener
item.description: PROVO, Utah (AP) - Richie Saunders scored a career-high 30 points and BYU cruised to a 76-56 victory over Arizona State on Tuesday in a Big 12 Conference opener.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Westbrook's triple-double leads Denver past Utah
item.description: DENVER (AP) - Russell Westbrook became the second player in NBA history to have a triple-double with zero turnovers and shoot 100% from the field and the free-throw line in the Denver Nuggets' 132-121 win over the Utah Jazz on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah's road winning streak ends in Seattle
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) - Jaden Schwartz had a goal and two assists for the Seattle Kraken, who pulled away in the third period for a 5-2 win against the Utah Hockey Club at Climate Pledge Arena on Monday. The loss ends Utah's seven-game road winning streak. “[Points] never come easy,” Schwartz said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Holiday Classic: Father Judge 80, Carlsbad 70
item.description: In the Third-Place game for the National Bracket of the 2024 Holiday Classic, Father Judge High School from Philadelphia, PA defeats Carlsbad 80-70.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Darnold gives Vikings another gem with career-high 377 yards in 27-25 win over Packers
item.description: The Minnesota Vikings were ready to start their postgame ritual in honor of a ninth consecutive victory before realizing Sam Darnold was still missing from the raucous locker room because he was finishing his interview with the TV broadcasting crew.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Moment of silence for former President Jimmy Carter held before the Falcons-Commanders game
item.description: Jimmy Carter was honored with a moment of silence before the Atlanta Falcons’ game at the Washington Commanders on Sunday night, hours after the 39th president of the United States died at the age of 100 in Plains, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: BYU crushes Colorado in Alamo Bowl, 36-14
item.description: SAN ANTONIO, Tex. (AP) - L.J. Martin rushed for 93 yards and two touchdowns, Jake Retzlaff passed for 151 yards, and No. 17 BYU beat 20th-ranked Colorado 36-14 in the Alamo Bowl on Saturday night. “Understanding that they have great players on the other side of the field," Martin said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: USU beats San Diego State on last-second shot, 67-66
item.description: SAN DIEGO, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah State men’s basketball earned its first road win over a ranked opponent in over three decades Saturday afternoon in front of a sellout crowd at Viejas Arena, taking down No. 20 San Diego State 67-66.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Point and Kucherov lead surging Lightning to 6-2 win over reeling Rangers
item.description: Brayden Point and Nikita Kucherov each had a goal and two assists to lead the Tampa Bay Lightning past the reeling New York Rangers 6-2 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shaq Barrett expected to re-sign with Bucs after being waived by Dolphins, reports say
item.description: Former Tampa Bay Buccaneers linebacker Shaq Barrett is expected to once again sign with the Bucs after he was waived by the Miami Dolphins, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tuch scores hat trick, Sabres beat Blackhawks 6-2 for 2nd straight win after 13-game skid
item.description: Alex Tuch scored a hat trick for the Buffalo Sabres in a 6-2 win against the Chicago Blackhawks on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
